mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism operates continuously whenever rate cuts occur, independent of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.2% as of May 2024 reflects recent easing trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations. The mechanism activates only when monthly payroll growth exceeds 150,000 jobs, indicating robust labor market momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149,893 thousand as of May 2024
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending strength and aggregate demand expansion. When real retail sales accelerate, corporate profit margins expand and equity valuations rise. This signal operates continuously as a trend indicator without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513,531 million dollars as of May 2024
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS momentum (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing strength. When industrial production declines sharply, it indicates demand weakness and supply-side stress, producing negative S&P 500 returns as investors reprrice growth expectations downward. The mechanism activates when monthly IP growth turns negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal direction reverses) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and payroll employment jointly signal construction sector strength and residential investment momentum. When both HOUST and PAYEMS accelerate simultaneously, multiplier effects amplify aggregate demand, producing positive equity returns. The mechanism requires both series to move in the same direction within a 1-month window.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change > 5% AND PAYEMS 3-month change > 300,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,228 thousand units and PAYEMS at 149,893 thousand as of May 2024
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 56% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (HOUST × PAYEMS) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) jointly determine real interest rates. When inflation accelerates while the Fed holds rates steady or cuts, real rates compress, supporting equity valuations. The mechanism activates when CPI year-over-year growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 252.876 and FEDFUNDS at 2.2% as of May 2024
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.15) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases signal labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, equity investors reprice earnings downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns. This mechanism is regime-specific to tightening or early-recession cycles.
regime_hint: recession onset or labor market deterioration phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when unemployment rises sharply.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales jointly capture aggregate demand strength. When both GDPC1 (quarterly) and RSAFS (monthly) show sustained acceleration, corporate earnings expand broadly, producing positive equity returns. The mechanism requires both demand components to strengthen simultaneously, indicating broad-based economic momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS 3-month change > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,671.65 billion chained 2017 dollars and RSAFS at 513,531 million dollars as of May 2024
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 57% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (GDPC1 × RSAFS) is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in any 2-year rolling regression.
